evidence: I think Ive seen every horror movie out there by now ---thousands at minimum! --Im a super-fan.
I am a walking dead fanatic, and I adore most apocolypse, infestation, terror themes anyway.
Unlike ANY other thriller I have ever seen-- tightly woven with subtlety that kept me on the edge of my seat.
I personally LOVED this movie and Im hard to please when it comes to horror, guts, gore, supernatural.
This held my interest the entire time and the scenes in the water had me lifting my legs while i was sitting in the chair!
I thought it was quite well done! I only fast forwarded a bit in the beginning as it was a bit slow, but once it got going -- I was all in.
I could barely get through half of it and fast forwarding was the only way I could do it.
The acting was laughable at best, and as others have commented, not once did think those sweet puppies would rip ANYONE limb to limb.
I am a walking dead fanatic, and I adore most apocolypse, infestation, terror themes anyway.
I personally thought this was an honest, real look at one woman's home and how they've been trying to verify what she's been saying.
I thought it was very well done! It had all the creepy elements of a classic, 'Oh Crap---its outside!' thrill.
I REALLY REALLY enjoyed this movie. It had everything, strained relationship between daughter and father, save the puppy!
I personally thought this was an honest, real look at one woman's home and how they've been trying to verify what she's been saying.
I thought this was tasteful, and well documented. FOR ME--- A WIN!

summary: The user has a strong preference for horror and thriller genres, often enjoying complex narratives with fast pacing. They are sensitive to production quality and have a high appreciation for sensory experiences, indicating a desire for engaging and well-crafted stories.
